essential_question: Of everything from Lessons 20–30, what still has to be automatic before the Final?
objective: Review Unit 03 — special right triangles, two-column proofs, congruence and similarity, transformations, and right-triangle trigonometry — through mixed cumulative practice.

content.prompt: Unit 03 was Lessons 20–30 — special right triangles, proofs, similarity, transformations, and trig. Without checking anything, which of those topics feels most automatic right now?

content.heading: Special triangles, proofs, similarity, transformations, trig
bullets: 45-45-90 and 30-60-90 special right triangles and their fixed side ratios.
Postulates, theorems, and two-column proofs (SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, HL).
Congruent versus similar triangles — CPCTC and AA similarity.
Right-triangle trigonometry — sine, cosine, tangent, and their reciprocals.

content.preview: Next week: the MUS Final Exam.

success_criteria.text: I can solve for a missing side in a special right triangle
I can name the correct proof shortcut (SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, HL) for a given pair of triangles
I can determine whether two triangles are similar using AA similarity
I can apply a transformation (reflection, rotation) to a point
I can compute a trig ratio (sine, cosine, tangent) from given side lengths

compass.note: Curriculum/Geometry/The Salas Compass/2026-W37 Year-End Final Review.md

questions.prompt: A 45-45-90 triangle has a leg of 6. Find the hypotenuse.
A 30-60-90 triangle has a hypotenuse of 20. Find the short leg.
Two triangles share an included angle between two pairs of congruent sides. Which proof shortcut applies?
questions.explain: Two sides and the INCLUDED angle between them congruent = SAS.

questions.prompt: Two triangles have two pairs of congruent angles. They must be…
questions.explain: AA similarity: two congruent angle pairs guarantee similar (not necessarily congruent) triangles.

questions.prompt: Rotate the point (4, 2) 90° counterclockwise about the origin. What are the new coordinates?
questions.explain: The rule for a 90° CCW rotation about the origin is (x,y)→(-y,x).

questions.prompt: In a right triangle, the side opposite a given angle is 6 and the side adjacent to it is 8. Find the tangent of that angle.
Exit: which Unit 03 idea from today's mix would you want one more example of before the Final?
